session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Land-Learn Integration 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the erk land command's learn workflow" 5→ - "debugging session discovery during land" 6→ - "working with session reporting in the land pipeline" 7→ - "learn plan creation" 8→ - "session discovery" 9→ - "empty learn plan" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "making session discovery failures block the land command" 12→ warning: "Session discovery uses fire-and-forget error resilience. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in try/except to prevent blocking land. Failures are reported as warnings, never errors." 13→ score: 6 14→ - action: "modifying learn plan skip guards in land_learn.py" 15→ warning: "Learn plan creation may skip silently when no sessions exist. Check land-learn-integration.md before modifying skip guards." 16→--- 17→ 18→# Land-Learn Integration 19→ 20→After landing a PR, `erk land` optionally creates a learn plan to capture implementation insights. The session discovery step reports what sessions are available for the learn workflow. 21→ 22→## Skip Guards 23→ 24→Learn plan creation skips in two scenarios: 25→ 26→1. **No sessions tracked at all** — e.g., manual CHANGELOG PRs or PRs without implementation sessions. The `all_session_ids` list is empty because no planning, implementation, or learn sessions were recorded. 27→ 28→2. **Sessions exist but XML extraction produces no content** — Sessions were tracked in GitHub metadata but preprocessing produced no usable XML (warmup sessions, empty sessions, or sessions that fail to parse). 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py, _create_learn_pr_impl -->
... [92 lines omitted] ...
99→## Fire-and-Forget Error Resilience 106→except Exception as exc: 107→ user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Could not create learn plan: {exc}") 110→This ensures that learn failures never block the primary `erk land` operation. A failed learn plan is a warning, not an error.

1→--- 2→title: Land-Learn Integration 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the erk land command's learn workflow" 5→ - "debugging session discovery during land" 6→ - "working with session reporting in the land pipeline" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "making session discovery failures block the land command" 9→ warning: "Session discovery uses fire-and-forget error resilience. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in try/except to prevent blocking land. Failures are reported as warnings, never errors." 10→ score: 6 11→--- 12→ 13→# Land-Learn Integration 14→ 15→After landing a PR, `erk land` optionally creates a learn plan to capture implementation insights. The session discovery step reports what sessions are available for the learn workflow. 16→ 17→## Session Discovery Logging 18→ 19→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py:209-305, _log_session_discovery --> 20→ 21→See `_log_session_discovery()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py`. This function reports session discovery results and returns a file map of preprocessed session XML. 22→ 23→### Return Value 24→ 25→Returns `dict[str, str]` mapping file paths to XML content. Each key follows the naming convention `.erk/impl-context/sessions/{type}-{session_id}.xml` (with `-part{N}` suffix for multi-chunk sessions). The dict feeds into `create_plan_draft_pr(extra_files=...)` to embed session XML in the learn plan PR diff. 26→ 27→### Output Format 28→ 29→The function produces a structured summary with per-session type badges, turn counts, duration, and compression stats. 30→
... [58 lines omitted] ...
62→## Fire-and-Forget Error Resilience 66→See `_create_learn_pr_with_sessions()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py` for the fire-and-forget wrapper. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in a try/except that catches all exceptions and emits a warning instead of raising. This ensures that learn failures never block the primary `erk land` operation. A failed learn plan is a warning, not an error.
session.assistant:
Now I have a clear picture. The plan says for B1 to use #8592 as structural base and layer #8513's additions. The #8592 version has Skip Guards, Session Discovery Pipeline (5-stage flow), Cycle Prevention, and expanded frontmatter. The #8513 version adds return type info, Session XML File Returns, File Inventory Logging, TUI Learn Plan Toast.
The merged version should combine both. The #8592 version is the better structural base. Let me now write all files efficiently. I'll start by writing the merged B1 file and all the new/updated files.
Let me now read the current master versions of files that need updating, then write all changes:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Activation Scripts 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "working with worktree environment setup" 7→ - "understanding .erk/activate.sh scripts" 8→ - "configuring post-create commands" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "removing the VIRTUAL_ENV guard from activation scripts" 11→ warning: "Guard prevents double activation when direnv and temp script both source activation. Removing it causes duplicate package installs." 12→--- 13→ 14→# Activation Scripts 15→ 16→## Overview 17→ 18→Erk generates `.erk/activate.sh` scripts in worktrees to provide a shell-sourceable environment setup. This enables opt-in shell integration where users explicitly source the script rather than relying on automatic shell manipulation. 19→ 20→## What the Script Does 21→ 22→When sourced, `.erk/activate.sh`: 23→ 24→1. CDs to the worktree directory 25→2. Creates `.venv` with `uv sync` if not present 26→3. Sources `.venv/bin/activate` if present 27→4. Loads `.env` file (using `set -a` for allexport) 28→5. Runs configured post-create commands 29→6. Displays activation confirmation 30→
... [87 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: View-Aware Command Filtering 3→read_when: 4→ - "registering a new TUI command with view-mode filtering" 5→ - "understanding how commands are filtered by view mode (plans, learn, objectives)" 6→ - "adding objective-specific commands to the command palette" 7→ - "implementing streaming commands in the TUI" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "registering a new TUI command without a view-mode predicate" 10→ warning: "Every command must use _is_plan_view() or _is_objectives_view() to prevent it from appearing in the wrong view. Commands without view predicates appear in all views." 11→ - action: "adding streaming commands without using the streaming operation pattern" 12→ warning: "Streaming ACTION commands need the streaming operation wrapper with status bar tracking. See streaming-output.md for the current pattern." 13→--- 14→ 15→# View-Aware Command Filtering 16→ 17→The TUI command palette filters commands by the active view mode. Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in the Objectives view. This filtering is implemented through view-mode predicates in the command registry. 18→ 19→## View Mode Predicates 20→ 21→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py:21-28 --> 22→ 23→Two predicates partition the command space by view mode: 24→ 25→<!-- See _is_plan_view and _is_objectives_view in src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py:21-28 --> 26→ 27→- `_is_plan_view(ctx)`: Returns `True` when not in Objectives view (i.e., Plans or Learn) — checks `ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES` 28→- `_is_objectives_view(ctx)`: Returns `True` when in Objectives view — checks `ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES` 29→ 30→The `CommandContext.view_mode` field carries the active `ViewMode` enum value. `MainListCommandProvider` reads this dynamically from `self._app._view_mode`, while `PlanCommandProvider` hardcodes `ViewMode.PLANS` since the detail modal is always in plan context.
... [87 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI Command Architecture 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new command to the TUI or desktop dashboard" 5→ - "understanding how command availability is determined" 6→ - "choosing which execution pattern a new command should use" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding a command without an availability predicate" 9→ warning: "Every command needs an is_available predicate based on PlanRowData field presence. Commands without predicates appear when they can't execute." 10→ - action: "adding an ACTION command that executes instantly" 11→ warning: "ACTION category implies mutative operations. Instant operations belong in OPEN or COPY categories." 12→last_audited: "2026-02-08 00:00 PT" 13→audit_result: edited 14→--- 15→ 16→# TUI Command Architecture 17→ 18→The TUI command system uses a data-driven registry pattern where commands declare their own availability based on `PlanRowData` field presence. This decouples command definitions from UI rendering, enabling the same registry to serve multiple frontends (TUI command palette, desktop toolbar). 19→ 20→## Why Data-Driven Availability 21→ 22→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py, get_all_commands --> 23→ 24→Commands don't check "can I run?" at execution time — they declare upfront what data they need via `is_available` predicates. This design was chosen because: 25→ 26→1. **Multiple consumers need the same logic.** The TUI command palette (`MainListCommandProvider`, `PlanCommandProvider`) and the desktop dashboard toolbar both need to know which actions are valid for a given row. Centralizing predicates in the registry avoids duplicating availability logic across frontends. 27→ 28→2. **Availability depends on `PlanRowData` nullability.** Each `PlanRowData` field is nullable for a reason (no PR yet, no workflow run, etc.). The predicates express which combination of non-null fields a command requires. This makes the availability contract explicit and testable. 29→ 30→3. **Commands that are always available use `lambda _: True`** — these are plan-level operations (close, prepare, dispatch) that only need the issue number, which is always present.
... [67 lines omitted] ...
58→**Anti-pattern**: Writing `is_available=lambda ctx: True` for a command that uses `ctx.row.pr_number`. The predicate will allow execution when `pr_number` is None, causing a runtime error. This is why the [three-layer null validation](adding-commands.md) pattern exists — the predicate is necessary but not sufficient.

1→--- 2→title: Stacked PR Indicator 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding or modifying PR indicators in the TUI dashboard" 5→ - "understanding blocking vs. informational indicators" 6→ - "working with stacked PR detection" 7→--- 8→ 9→# Stacked PR Indicator 10→ 11→The pancake emoji (🥞) indicates a stacked PR in the TUI dashboard lifecycle column. It appears when a plan's PR targets a branch other than master/main. 12→ 13→## Dual-Source Detection 14→ 15→Stacked status is detected from two sources with a fallback strategy: 16→ 17→1. **Primary: Graphite `get_parent_branch()`** — When the PR head branch is known, the branch manager's `get_parent_branch()` is consulted first. If it returns a parent branch not in `("master", "main")`, the PR is stacked. Graphite is authoritative because it reflects the actual stack topology. 18→ 19→2. **Fallback: GitHub `base_ref_name`** — Only when Graphite returns `None` (branch not tracked locally). The `base_ref_name` field on `PullRequestInfo` is checked against `("master", "main")`. 20→ 21→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py, RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data --> 22→ 23→See `RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data()` in `real.py` for the dual-source detection logic. 24→ 25→## Stale Data Handling 26→ 27→Graphite is authoritative because GitHub's `base_ref_name` can become stale. When a parent PR merges, GitHub does not always update the child PR's target branch immediately — the child may still show the old (now-merged) branch as its base. Graphite's local branch tracking reflects the actual stack state, making it the more reliable source for stacking detection. 28→ 29→## Indicator Ordering 30→
... [42 lines omitted] ...
41→- **Blocking indicators** (CI failures, unresolved reviews, merge conflicts) prevent the rocket emoji (🚀) from appearing in the implemented stage.

1→--- 2→title: TUI Architecture Overview 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding TUI structure" 5→ - "implementing TUI components" 6→ - "working with TUI data providers" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "reusing same DOM element id across loading/empty/content states" 9→ warning: "query_one() returns wrong element silently when id is reused across lifecycle phases. Use unique IDs per phase." 10→ - action: "calling widget methods directly from @work(thread=True) background threads" 11→ warning: "Direct widget calls from background threads cause silent UI corruption. Must use self.app.call_from_thread(callback, ...)." 12→ - action: "extending PlanDataProvider ABC" 13→ warning: "Requires 3-file update: abc.py + real.py + fake.py. Fake must initialize new dict in __init__. Missing init causes AttributeError at test time." 14→ - action: "adding a DataTable column with add_column(key=...)" 15→ warning: "Column key is a data binding contract — must match data field name. Silent failure when mismatched." 16→ - action: "moving @on decorated event handlers to a mixin" 17→ warning: "Textual's _MessagePumpMeta only scans class.__dict__, not inherited methods. Event handlers on mixins are silently ignored." 18→last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT" 19→audit_result: clean 20→--- 21→ 22→# TUI Architecture Overview 23→ 24→The erk TUI is built with Textual and follows a layered architecture separating data fetching, filtering, and rendering. 25→ 26→## Directory Structure 27→ 28→``` 29→src/erk/tui/ 30→├── app.py # Main Textual App (ErkDashApp)
... [190 lines omitted] ...
126→Use fake providers instead of mocking. `FakePlanDataProvider` lives in `erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake` and accepts keyword-only arguments (`plans`, `clipboard`, `browser`, `repo_root`, `fetch_error`). The `make_plan_row()` helper in the same module creates test `PlanRowData` instances with sensible defaults.

1→--- 2→title: CI Gitignored Directory Commit Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with .erk/impl-context/ in CI workflows" 5→ - "force-adding gitignored files in GitHub Actions" 6→ - "debugging why .erk/impl-context/ appears in PR diffs" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "committing .erk/impl-context/ without git add -f" 9→ warning: "The directory is gitignored. Use git add -f .erk/impl-context to force-add it. Without -f, git silently skips the directory." 10→ score: 5 11→--- 12→ 13→# CI Gitignored Directory Commit Patterns 14→ 15→`.erk/impl-context/` is gitignored but needs temporary git tracking during CI workflows. This requires force-add and explicit cleanup. 16→ 17→## Three-Phase Lifecycle 18→ 19→### Phase 1: Force-Add During Setup 20→ 21→<!-- Source: .github/workflows/plan-implement.yml, Phase 1 setup step --> 22→ 23→The setup step creates the implementation context, force-adds it to git tracking, and pushes to the remote branch. `git add -f` overrides `.gitignore` to track the directory. This creates a committed snapshot that the implementation agent reads from. 24→ 25→### Phase 2: Remove Before Implementation 26→ 27→<!-- Source: .github/workflows/plan-implement.yml, cleanup step --> 28→ 29→The cleanup step in `plan-implement.yml` (lines 406-409 and similar patterns) uses `git rm -r --cached` to remove from git tracking while preserving files on disk. The implementation agent still reads the local files, but they no longer appear in the PR diff. This is idempotent — safe to run even if already cleaned up. 30→
... [38 lines omitted] ...
41→| `git ls-files --error-unmatch .erk/impl-context/` | Check if directory is tracked (exit 1 if not) | 45→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_update_pr_body.py, plan-header error handling --> 47→When rewriting PR bodies in CI, the plan-header metadata block must be preserved. The error handling in `ci_update_pr_body.py` (around lines 266-277) validates that the plan-header metadata block exists, using a `UpdateError` discriminated union with a specific `plan-header-not-found` variant to signal metadata loss in previous CI steps.

1→--- 2→title: Autofix Job Needs 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the autofix job's needs list in ci.yml" 5→ - "adding a new CI job that might block autofix" 6→ - "understanding autofix dependency design" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "removing test jobs from autofix's needs list" 9→ warning: "Autofix depends on ALL validation jobs including unit-tests and integration-tests. It needs full failure context to attempt intelligent fixes. See the needs array in ci.yml." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Autofix Job Needs 13→ 14→## Current Design 15→ 16→The autofix job depends on **all** validation jobs, including test jobs. This gives the autofix agent full failure context to attempt intelligent fixes. 17→ 18→<!-- Source: .github/workflows/ci.yml, autofix job needs array --> 19→ 20→See the `autofix` job's `needs` array in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` for the authoritative list: 21→ 22→- format, lint, fix-formatting, docs-check, ty, unit-tests, integration-tests 23→ 24→The job uses `always()` in its `if` condition, so it runs even when upstream jobs fail. The dependency list controls **ordering** (autofix waits for all jobs to finish) rather than **gating** (autofix still runs on failure). 25→ 26→## Why All Jobs Are Included 27→ 28→The autofix agent uses Claude Code with failure context from all jobs. Including test jobs in `needs` ensures: 29→ 30→1. All job results are available via `needs.<job>.result`
... [31 lines omitted] ...
31→2. The agent sees both style failures AND test failures in one pass 32→3. Error messages from test jobs are collected and passed to Claude 40→| format (ruff format) | Yes | Failure context for auto-fix | 41→| lint (ruff check) | Yes | Failure context for auto-fix | 43→| docs-check | Yes | Failure context for docs-sync | 44→| ty (type check) | Yes | Failure context for type fixes | 45→| unit-tests | Yes | Failure context for intelligent fixes | 46→| integration-tests | Yes | Failure context for intelligent fixes | 54→- [CI Iteration Pattern](ci-iteration.md) - Iterative fixing of CI failures

1→--- 2→title: ErkPackageInfo Value Object 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with ErkPackageInfo or bundled paths" 5→ - "understanding is_in_erk_repo detection" 6→ - "writing tests that need ErkPackageInfo" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating ErkPackageInfo directly in production code" 9→ warning: "Use ErkPackageInfo.from_project_dir(). Direct construction is for tests only." 10→last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT" 11→audit_result: clean 12→--- 13→ 14→# ErkPackageInfo Value Object 15→ 16→`ErkPackageInfo` consolidates package-level metadata into a single frozen dataclass, providing two construction paths: one for production and one for tests. 17→ 18→## Class Definition 19→ 20→<!-- Source: src/erk/artifacts/paths.py, ErkPackageInfo --> 21→ 22→See `ErkPackageInfo` in `src/erk/artifacts/paths.py`. Frozen dataclass with fields: `in_erk_repo`, `bundled_claude_dir`, `bundled_github_dir`, `bundled_erk_dir`, `current_version`. 23→ 24→## Two-Constructor Pattern 25→ 26→### Production: from_project_dir() 27→ 28→<!-- Source: src/erk/artifacts/paths.py, ErkPackageInfo.from_project_dir --> 29→ 30→See `ErkPackageInfo.from_project_dir()` in `src/erk/artifacts/paths.py`. Calls `is_in_erk_repo()`, `get_bundled_claude_dir()`, `get_bundled_github_dir()`, `get_bundled_erk_dir()`, and `get_current_version()` to fully populate all fields. Uses inline imports to avoid circular dependencies.
... [36 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: GitHub Review Decision Display 3→read_when: 4→ - "implementing review decision indicators in the TUI" 5→ - "understanding how PR review status flows from GraphQL to the TUI display" 6→ - "debugging missing or incorrect review decision indicators" 7→--- 8→ 9→# GitHub Review Decision Display 10→ 11→The TUI plan list displays review decision status as emoji indicators on plans in the `review` lifecycle stage. This document describes the complete pipeline from GraphQL to display. 12→ 13→## Pipeline 14→ 15→``` 16→GraphQL reviewDecision field 17→ ↓ 18→PullRequestInfo.review_decision: str | None 19→ ↓ 20→real.py: selected_pr.review_decision → pr_review_decision 21→ ↓ 22→compute_status_indicators(review_decision=pr_review_decision) 23→ ↓ 24→TUI plan list "sts" column with emoji indicators 25→``` 26→ 27→## GraphQL Source 28→ 29→The `reviewDecision` field is fetched in three queries in `graphql_queries.py` (search for `reviewDecision` to locate): 30→
... [46 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI Status Indicators 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with status indicators in TUI dashboard" 5→ - "adding new emoji indicators to plan display" 6→ - "modifying the sts column" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding emoji with Unicode variation selector (\\ufe0f) to status indicators" 9→ warning: "Variation selector forces double-wide rendering in terminals, breaking column alignment. Current safe emoji: 🥞 🚧 👀 💥 ✔ ❌ 🚀. Test any new emoji in terminal before adding." 10→ score: 5 11→ - action: "extracting status indicators from the lifecycle display string" 12→ warning: "Indicators are computed from RAW PR state fields (is_draft, has_conflicts, review_decision), NOT extracted from lifecycle display. Use compute_status_indicators() for standalone display, format_lifecycle_with_status() for inline." 13→ score: 4 14→--- 15→ 16→# TUI Status Indicators 17→ 18→Status indicators show PR state as emoji in the TUI dashboard. They are computed from raw PR state fields, not extracted from the lifecycle display string. 19→ 20→## Two Public Functions 21→ 22→**Location:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/lifecycle.py` 23→ 24→### compute_status_indicators() — Standalone Display 25→ 26→Used for the "sts" column in the dashboard. Returns space-joined emoji string or "-" when empty. 27→ 28→```python 29→compute_status_indicators( 30→ lifecycle_display="[cyan]impl[/cyan]",

session.assistant:
Now I have a clear picture. Most source branch files already contain the exact final content we need. The strategy:
1. For files that already match what's on master: skip (A1, A2, A6, B2, B4, C2, C3) 2. For NEW files: write content from source branches directly 3. For UPDATES: the source branch versions already contain the updates applied on top of master — use them directly
The key insight is that each source branch version of an updated file already IS the final version we want (it was the content of that PR). Let me now compare current master versions against the source branch versions to confirm they contain the needed changes, then write them all.
Let me now write all the files that need creating or updating. I'll do this in batches:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: # Write A3: inference-hoisting.md - copy from #8513 source (has Plan Summary Hoisting sect...[truncated 227 chars]...erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/worktree-dispatch-detection.md 2>/dev/null echo "A4: $?"

session.tool_result: A3 done A4: 1
session.assistant: The worktree file doesn't exist in /tmp because git show piped to a variable. Let me use the content I already retrieved:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: # The git show commands earlier wrote to /tmp files - check them ls -la /tmp/worktree-disp...[truncated 236 chars]...tch-detection.md 2>/dev/null echo "Written: $?" ls -la /tmp/worktree-dispatch-detection.md

session.assistant: Wait — the earlier batch command with redirects worked fine (all returned 0). The issue is the copy destination was wrong. Let me re-pull the files that had issues and write them all properly:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: # Write all NEW files by copying from /tmp where git show earlier wrote them # A4: worktre...[truncated 1922 chars]...md /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/sessions/session-accumulation.md && echo "E7 OK"

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param:
# A3: inference-hoisting.md already done above
# B1: land-learn-integration.md - use #859...[truncated 1501 chars]...d-8567.md /home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md && echo "E8b OK"

session.tool_result: B1 base OK B3 OK C1a OK C1b OK C4 OK C5 OK D1 OK D2 OK E1 OK E8a OK E8b OK
session.assistant: Now I need to handle B1 (the overlap merge). The #8592 version is the structural base, but it needs the #8513 additions layered in. Let me check what #8513 adds that #8592 doesn't have:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: # Check what's in #8513 that's not in #8592 diff /tmp/land-learn-8592.md /tmp/land-learn-8513.md | head -100

session.tool_result:
--- /tmp/land-learn-8592.md 2026-03-02 17:58:26.902485419 +0000 +++ /tmp/land-learn-8513.md 2026-03-02 17:58:26.904485433 +0000 @@ -4,113 +4,79 @@ - "modifying the erk land command's learn workflow" - "debugging session discovery during land" - "working with session reporting in the land pipeline" - - "learn plan creation" - - "session discovery" - - "empty learn plan" tripwires: - action: "making session discovery failures block the land command" warning: "Session discovery uses fire-and-forget error resilience. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in try/except to prevent blocking land. Failures are reported as warnings, never errors." score: 6 - - action: "modifying learn plan skip guards in land_learn.py" - warning: "Learn plan creation may skip silently when no sessions exist. Check land-learn-integration.md before modifying skip guards." ---
# Land-Learn Integration
After landing a PR, `erk land` optionally creates a learn plan to capture implementation insights. The session discovery step reports what sessions are available for the learn workflow.
-## Skip Guards - -Learn plan creation skips in two scenarios: - -1. **No sessions tracked at all** — e.g., manual CHANGELOG PRs or PRs without implementation sessions. The `all_session_ids` list is empty because no planning, implementation, or learn sessions were recorded. - -2. **Sessions exist but XML extraction produces no content** — Sessions were tracked in GitHub metadata but preprocessing produced no usable XML (warmup sessions, empty sessions, or sessions that fail to parse). - -<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py, _create_learn_pr_impl -->
... [121 lines omitted] ...
+## Fire-and-Forget Error Resilience +See `_create_learn_pr_with_sessions()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py` for the fire-and-forget wrapper. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in a try/except that catches all exceptions and emits a warning instead of raising. This ensures that learn failures never block the primary `erk land` operation. A failed learn plan is a warning, not an error. -## Fire-and-Forget Error Resilience -except Exception as exc: - user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Could not create learn plan: {exc}") -This ensures that learn failures never block the primary `erk land` operation. A failed learn plan is a warning, not an error.
session.assistant: I see the diff clearly. The #8592 version has richer structural content (Skip Guards, Session Discovery Pipeline 5-stage flow, Cycle Prevention) while #8513 has unique additions (Return Value, Session XML File Returns, File Inventory Logging, TUI Learn Plan Toast). I need to merge these. Let me create the merged version:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Land-Learn Integration 3→read_when: 4→ - "modifying the erk land command's learn workflow" 5→ - "debugging session discovery during land" 6→ - "working with session reporting in the land pipeline" 7→ - "learn plan creation" 8→ - "session discovery" 9→ - "empty learn plan" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "making session discovery failures block the land command" 12→ warning: "Session discovery uses fire-and-forget error resilience. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in try/except to prevent blocking land. Failures are reported as warnings, never errors." 13→ score: 6 14→ - action: "modifying learn plan skip guards in land_learn.py" 15→ warning: "Learn plan creation may skip silently when no sessions exist. Check land-learn-integration.md before modifying skip guards." 16→--- 17→ 18→# Land-Learn Integration 19→ 20→After landing a PR, `erk land` optionally creates a learn plan to capture implementation insights. The session discovery step reports what sessions are available for the learn workflow. 21→ 22→## Skip Guards 23→ 24→Learn plan creation skips in two scenarios: 25→ 26→1. **No sessions tracked at all** — e.g., manual CHANGELOG PRs or PRs without implementation sessions. The `all_session_ids` list is empty because no planning, implementation, or learn sessions were recorded. 27→ 28→2. **Sessions exist but XML extraction produces no content** — Sessions were tracked in GitHub metadata but preprocessing produced no usable XML (warmup sessions, empty sessions, or sessions that fail to parse). 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py, _create_learn_pr_impl -->
... [92 lines omitted] ...
99→## Fire-and-Forget Error Resilience 106→except Exception as exc: 107→ user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Could not create learn plan: {exc}") 110→This ensures that learn failures never block the primary `erk land` operation. A failed learn plan is a warning, not an error.

Since #8592 content (Launch Keys) is already on master, I need to check if the #8529 changes (p shortcut for view-aware dispatch) are still needed:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: # Check what #8529 changed in keyboard-shortcuts vs master diff <(git show master:docs/learned/tui/keyboard-shortcuts.md) /tmp/keyboard-8529.md | head -40

session.tool_result:
Exit code 1 --- /dev/fd/63 2026-03-02 18:00:21.664146925 +0000 +++ /tmp/keyboard-8529.md 2026-03-02 17:58:26.955485769 +0000 @@ -28,27 +28,17 @@
**Filters:** o (objective), t (stack), / (text filter), s (sort)
-**Item Actions:** enter/space (detail), p (open PR), n (open run), c (comments), h (checks), v (view plan body), l (launch menu), i (implement) +**Item Actions:** enter/space (detail), p (open PR / open objective, view-aware), n (open run), c (comments), h (checks), v (view plan body), l (launch menu), i (implement)
**System:** ctrl+p (command palette), r (refresh), ? (help), q/escape (quit)
-## Launch Keys - -Launch keys are defined in `CommandDefinition.launch_key` fields in `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`, not a separate LAUNCH_KEYS dict. See [TUI Command Registration](tui-command-registration.md) for the full table of current assignments. - -Key highlights: - -- Plan view: c (close), d (dispatch), l (land), r (rebase), a (address), w (rewrite), m (cmux) -- Objective view: c (close), s (one-shot), k (check) -- Rebase changed from `f` to `r` in PR #8560 for mnemonic consistency - ## Naming Convention
Action methods follow the pattern `action_<verb>_<noun>`:
-- `action_open_pr` -- opens the PR in browser -- `action_open_run` -- opens the GitHub Actions run URL -- `action_view_comments` -- opens the comments modal +- `action_open_pr` — opens the PR in browser
... [14 lines omitted] ...

So the merged version should keep BOTH the p description update AND the Launch Keys section. Let me apply just the `p` description change to the current master version:

The bindings include both visible shortcuts (shown in help) and hidden power-user shortcuts. 22→ 23→## Key Groups 24→ 25→**Navigation:** j/k (vim-style), escape (quit) 26→ 27→**View Switching:** 1/2/3 (direct), left/right (cycle) 28→ 29→**Filters:** o (objective), t (stack), / (text filter), s (sort) 30→
